name: Robotics / autonomous system
summary: A cyber-physical system that perceives its environment, plans, and actuates in the physical world under real-time control, operating autonomously or semi-autonomously near people and property. Safety-critical, real-time, uncertainty-bound.

concerns: hazard identification and risk assessment for physical harm (crushing, impact, pinch, cutting) per ISO 12100 / ISO 10218
emergency stop (Category 0/1) and protective stop reachable, independent of the main controller, and de-energising to a safe state

collaborative-operation safety modes: safety-rated monitored stop, hand-guiding, speed & separation monitoring, and power-&-force limiting (ISO/TS 15066, integrated in ISO 10218-2:2025) with biomechanical force/pressure limits
safety-rated functions to a required Performance Level / SIL (ISO 13849 / IEC 61508 / IEC 62061); safety-rated soft limits on speed, force, workspace/zones
safe-state definition on power loss, sensor loss, comms loss, or software fault; brakes hold on de-energisation
service/personal-care robots operating among the public (ISO 13482) where guarding is not possible
questions: Have physical hazards been identified with mitigations, and is a safe state defined for power/sensor/comms/software faults?
Is an independent emergency/protective stop specified that de-energises to a safe state?
For human-collaborative operation, are speed-&-separation and power-&-force limits (ISO/TS 15066 biomechanical limits) and the required Performance Level / SIL specified?

concerns: hard real-time control-loop determinism: cycle rate, worst-case latency, and jitter bounds for the servo/control loop
sensor-to-actuation end-to-end latency budget (perception → planning → control)
deterministic scheduling and guaranteed compute/GPU headroom for perception under peak scene complexity
real-time bus timing (EtherCAT/CAN) and missed-deadline handling
questions: Are the control-loop rate, worst-case latency, and jitter bounds specified (hard real-time)?
Is the end-to-end sensor-to-actuation latency budget quantified?
Is behaviour on missed deadlines / compute overload under peak perception load defined?

concerns: perception, localization/SLAM, and mapping accuracy under real-world sensing
motion/path planning correctness: collision-free trajectories, kinematic/dynamic limit adherence, and reachability
sensor fusion across lidar/camera/IMU/encoders with defined accuracy and failure handling for degraded/occluded sensors
behaviour under environmental uncertainty: unknown obstacles, dynamic actors, out-of-distribution scenes, and graceful task abort
calibration (intrinsic/extrinsic, hand-eye) and its accuracy/validity requirements
questions: Are perception/localization accuracy targets and behaviour under degraded/occluded sensors specified?
Are planning correctness (collision-free, within kinematic/dynamic limits) requirements defined?
Is behaviour under environmental uncertainty (unknown/dynamic obstacles, OOD scenes) and calibration validity specified?

concerns: fault detection, isolation and recovery (FDIR) for sensor, actuator, and compute faults
graceful degradation: reduced-speed/limp-home/controlled-stop modes when a subsystem fails
redundancy for safety- or mission-critical sensing/compute where a single failure is unacceptable
MTBF and unattended-operation duration; recovery after fault without hazard
questions: Is fault detection/isolation/recovery for sensor, actuator, and compute faults specified?
Are degraded modes (reduced-speed, controlled-stop, limp-home) defined per subsystem failure?
Is redundancy required for any single-point-of-failure critical to safety or mission?

concerns: command-and-control integrity: authenticated, tamper-proof motion commands (a spoofed command is a safety event)
teleoperation/remote-access channel security and session authentication
cybersecurity of the safety-relevant control path (now in scope of ISO 10218:2025)
secure software/firmware update of the controller without weakening safety functions
questions: Are motion/command channels authenticated and integrity-protected against spoofing (a security-safety coupling)?
Is remote/teleoperation access secured and authenticated?
Is cybersecurity of the safety-relevant control path addressed?

concerns: human-robot interaction clarity: intent signalling (lights/sound/motion) so bystanders anticipate motion
operator interfaces for mode selection, jogging, recovery, and clear status/fault indication
teleoperation fallback usability and low-latency operator feedback when autonomy disengages
training/skill assumptions for operators and safe hand-over between autonomy and human
questions: Does the robot signal intent/status so nearby humans can anticipate its motion?
Are operator interfaces for mode, recovery, and fault indication specified?
Is a teleoperation/manual fallback and safe autonomy↔human handover defined?

concerns: operating-environment envelope: floor/terrain, lighting, temperature, dust/water (IP rating), EMI
commissioning, calibration, and site setup (maps, zones, fiducials) as an operational procedure
fleet coordination/traffic management where multiple robots share a space
field maintenance, spare handling, and recharge/refuel/battery-swap logistics
questions: Is the operating environment (terrain, lighting, temperature, IP rating, EMI) specified?
Are commissioning/calibration/site-setup procedures defined?
Is multi-robot coordination and energy/maintenance logistics addressed?

concerns: black-box / flight-recorder logging of sensor+command+state for incident reconstruction and safety audits
simulation and hardware-in-the-loop testability for autonomy validation before field release
diagnosability of intermittent physical faults; component replaceability and re-calibration workflow
observability of autonomy internals (planner decisions, confidence) for debugging non-deterministic behaviour
questions: Is black-box logging of sensor/command/state for incident reconstruction required?
Are simulation/HIL testability and autonomy-validation requirements specified?
Is diagnosability/observability of autonomy decisions and recalibration workflow addressed?

concerns: sensor/actuator hardware interfaces and driver compatibility (CAN, EtherCAT, ROS/ROS 2 message contracts)
interoperability with plant/site systems (MES/WMS, fleet managers, safety PLCs)
map/coordinate-frame and data-format interoperability across tools
questions: Are sensor/actuator interfaces and fieldbus/middleware (CAN/EtherCAT/ROS) contracts specified?
Is integration with plant/site systems (MES/WMS, safety PLC, fleet manager) defined?
